title: adenophorus oahuensis
intro: Adenophorus oahuensis, also known as Ma'o hau hele, is a flowering plant native to Hawaii and a member of the club moss family. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Adenophorus oahuensis are believed to include wound healing, reduction of fever, and relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Adenophorus oahuensis can be used as a tea, tincture, or topical application to harness its medicinal properties.
